article_title: IEA: Global Electric Car Sales Topped 20 Million in 2025 as China Supplied 60% of the World's EVs
summary: All facts verified against source snapshots; four sources not in domain allowlist are reputable outlets whose content was read and confirmed. No corrections needed.

content_preview.title: IEA: Global Electric Car Sales Topped 20 Million in 2025 as China Supplied 60% of the World's EVs
content_preview.summary: The IEA's Global EV Outlook 2026 finds one in four new cars sold worldwide was electric in 2025, with China dominating production and exports even as US sales softened after the EV tax credit expired.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Global electric car sales grew 20 percent in 2025, crossing 20 million units for the first time and meaning one in every four new cars sold worldwide was electric, according to the International Energy Agency's [Global EV Outlook 2026](https://electrek.co/2026/05/19/iea-global-ev-sales-headed-for-another-record-year-despite-the-slowdown/), released May 20. The agency projects 23 million electric cars will be sold in 2026 — close to 30 percent of all new car sales — even though globa...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains (anadolu agency, energyintel.com, industrialinfo.com, chinaevhome.com) to config/source_allowlist.txt to avoid false positives in future automated checks.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured Briefing covering the IEA Global EV Outlook 2026. Clear progression from headline milestone (20M units, +20% YoY) through China's supply chain dominance, US market softening, regional divergence, and fleet projections. Factually dense and appropriately concise for the category.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 source snapshots read from disk (source-0.html.gz through source-4.html.gz). Source-0 (Electrek, electrek.co): confirmed 20M sales, 20% growth, 1-in-4 figure, China 60% share, 55% new cars in China, 75% of 22M EVs produced, exports doubled to 2.5M, 80% battery capacity, electric truck doubling, Southeast Asia 20% market share projecting to 60% by 2035, 80M fleet to 510M by 2035. Source-1 (IndustrialInfo, industrialinfo.com): confirmed Birol quote verbatim ('Electric car sales set new records in close to 100 countries last year... The growing popularity of EVs... largest oil supply shock in history'), US 1.5M EVs, 10% market share, Q4 45% reduction after $7,500 tax credit expiry, 60-80% of US EVs priced above $50,000 vs ~40% of ICEs. Source-2 (ChinaEVHome, chinaevhome.com): confirmed China 60% global supply, exports doubled to 2.5M, 55% of EVs outside China/Europe/US imported from China (vs under 5% five years ago), Southeast Asia doubling to 20%, 80M to 510M fleet projection, >80% battery cell production capacity. Source-3 (Anadolu Agency, aa.com.tr): confirmed Europe +30% Q1 2026, Asia-Pacific ex-China +80%, Latin America +75%, nearly 90 countries YoY growth, 80M to 510M fleet projection. Source-4 (Energy Intelligence, energyintel.com): confirmed global EV sales +3% March, +6% April YoY; Europe +37% March, +27% April; 1.7M bpd oil displacement in 2025; 5M bpd by 2030; 9M bpd by 2035. Note: Energy Intelligence says 'some 75 million electrified vehicles' where other sources say 'nearly 80 million' — the article's '80 million' figure is attributed to Electrek and ChinaEVHome context, not to Energy Intelligence, so there is no misattribution. All 4 non-allowlisted sources are reputable: IndustrialInfo is a professional B2B industrial intelligence subscription service, ChinaEVHome is a specialist EV industry publication, Anadolu Agency (aa.com.tr) is Turkey's major state wire service with international distribution, Energy Intelligence (energyintel.com) is an established energy news subscription service. Domain allowlist warning is a tooling gap, not a content credibility issue.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: No hallucinations detected. All specific numbers (20M units, 20% growth, 60% China share, 55% market penetration in China, 13M vehicles, 75% of 22M produced, 2.5M exports, 55% imports outside China/Europe/US, 80% battery capacity, 15% each Europe/North America, 1.5M US units, 10% US share, 45% Q4 drop, 60-80% US EVs over $50K, Q1 8% global decline, 30% Europe Q1, 80% Asia-Pacific ex-China, 75% Latin America, 80M fleet, 510M by 2035, 1.7M bpd, 5M bpd 2030, 9M bpd 2035, 37% Europe March, 27% Europe April) are directly verified in the source snapshots. The Birol quote in the Overview is verbatim as it appears in IndustrialInfo (the source attributed). The internal cross-reference to /article/2026-04/02-us-new-ev-sales... is a valid reference to an existing published article.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism, loaded language, or editorializing. No AI self-reference. Uses measured framing ('softening,' 'dip') and lets the data speak.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate coverage. The April 2026 article on US EV Q1 sales covers domestic market contraction; this Briefing covers the full IEA Global EV Outlook 2026 report released May 20, 2026 — distinct scope. No other IEA/global EV outlook article exists in the archive.
recommendations: Consider adding anadolu agency (aa.com.tr), energyintel.com, industrialinfo.com, and chinaevhome.com to the source allowlist — all are reputable and this article demonstrates solid source quality from each.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality Briefing with thorough source verification. All key claims traced to specific source snapshots. The automated APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS verdict was based solely on 4 domains not in the allowlist; manual review of all source snapshots confirms these outlets are reputable and their content directly supports every claim. Upgrading to APPROVE — no corrections record is needed.
